heading: Applications; eligibility
label: 1070g–2 Applications; eligibility

L. 117–49, § 5135 Stat. 405Oct. 13, 2021“In carrying out this Act [see Short Title of 2021 Amendment note set out under ] and any amendments made by this Act, or any regulations promulgated under this Act or under such amendments, the Secretary of Education may waive the application of—section 1001 of this title“(1)subchapter I of chapter 35 of title 44, United States Code (commonly known as the ‘Paperwork Reduction Act’);“(2)the master calendar requirements under section 482 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ();20 U.S.C. 1089“(3)negotiated rulemaking under section 492 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (); and20 U.S.C. 1098a“(4)the requirement to publish the notices related to the system of records of the agency before implementation required under paragraphs (4) and (11) of , United States Code (commonly known as the ‘Privacy Act of 1974’), except that the notices shall be published not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act [].”section 552a(e) of title 5Oct. 13, 2021

L. 114–95, title IX, § 9214(a)129 Stat. 2160Dec. 10, 2015“Beginning on the date of enactment of this Act []—Dec. 10, 2015“(1)any reference in sections 420N, 428J, 428K, and 460 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (, 1078–10, 1078–11, and 1087j) to the term ‘highly qualified’ as defined in section 9101 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [now section 8101, ] shall be treated as a reference to such term under such section 9101 as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of this Act []; and20 U.S.C. 1070g–220 U.S.C. 7801Dec. 10, 2015“(2)any reference in section 6112 of the America COMPETES Act ([former] ), section 553 of the America COMPETES Reauthorization Act of 2010 (), and section 9 of the National Science Foundation Authorization Act of 2002 (), to ‘highly qualified’, as defined in section 9101 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [now section 8101, ], with respect to a teacher, means that the teacher meets applicable State certification and licensure requirements, including any requirements for certification obtained through alternative routes to certification.”20 U.S.C. 981220 U.S.C. 990342 U.S.C. 1862n20 U.S.C. 7801

children.heading: Filing required
children.label: 1 Filing required
children.text: The Secretary shall periodically set dates by which teacher candidates shall file applications for grants under this subpart. Each teacher candidate desiring a grant under this subpart for any year shall file an application containing such information and assurances as the Secretary may determine necessary to enable the Secretary to carry out the functions and responsibilities of this subpart.

children.label: i
children.text: the student is an eligible student for purposes of ;section 1091 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: has a grade point average that is determined, under standards prescribed by the Secretary, to be comparable to a 3.25 average on a zero to 4.0 scale, except that, if the student is in the first year of a program of undergraduate education, such grade point average shall be determined on the basis of the student’s cumulative secondary school grade point average; or

children.label: II
children.text: displayed high academic aptitude by receiving a score above the 75th percentile on at least one of the batteries in an undergraduate, post-baccalaureate, or graduate school admissions test; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the student is completing coursework and other requirements necessary to begin a career in teaching, or plans to complete such coursework and requirements prior to graduating; or

children.label: i
children.text: the applicant is a teacher or a retiree from another occupation with expertise in a field in which there is a shortage of teachers, such as mathematics, science, special education, English language acquisition, or another high-need subject; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the applicant is or was a teacher who is using high-quality alternative certification routes, such as Teach for America, to get certified.

children.label: A
children.text: serve as a full-time teacher for a total of not less than 4 academic years within 8 years after completing the course of study for which the applicant received a TEACH Grant under this subpart (referred to in this section as the “service obligation window”);

children.label: B
children.text: teach in a school described in ;section 1087ee(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: mathematics;

children.label: ii
children.text: science;

children.label: iii
children.text: a foreign language;

children.label: iv
children.text: bilingual education;

children.label: v
children.text: special education;

children.label: vi
children.text: as a reading specialist; or

children.label: vii
children.text: another field or geographic area documented as high-need by the Federal Government, State government, or local educational agency, and approved by the Secretary;

children.label: D
children.text: submit a certification of employment by the chief administrative officer of the school in accordance with subsection (d)(5); and

children.label: E
children.text: meet all State certification requirements for teaching (which may include meeting such requirements through a certification obtained through alternative routes to teaching);

in the event that the applicant is determined to have failed or refused to carry out such service obligation, the sum of the amounts of any TEACH Grants received by such applicant will be treated as a loan and collected from the applicant in accordance with subsection (c) and the regulations thereunder; and

contains, or is accompanied by, a plain-language disclosure form developed by the Secretary that clearly describes the nature of the TEACH Grant award, the service obligation, and the loan repayment requirements that are the consequence of the failure to complete the service obligation.

children.heading: Repayment for failure to complete service
children.label: c Repayment for failure to complete service

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the event that any recipient of a grant under this subpart fails or refuses to comply with the service obligation in the agreement under subsection (b), the sum of the amounts of any TEACH Grants received by such recipient shall, upon a determination of such a failure or refusal in such service obligation, be treated as a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan under part D, and shall be subject to repayment, together with interest thereon accruing from the date of the grant award, in accordance with terms and conditions specified by the Secretary in regulations under this subpart.

children.heading: Request to reconsider
children.label: A Request to reconsider
children.text: In any case where the Secretary has determined that a recipient of a grant under this subpart has failed or refused to comply with the service obligation in the agreement under subsection (b) and has converted the grant into a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan under part D in accordance with paragraph (1), (including a TEACH Grant converted to a loan prior to , and including cases where such loans have been fully or partially paid), the recipient may request that the Secretary reconsider such initial determination and may submit additional information to demonstrate satisfaction of the service obligation. Upon receipt of such a request, the Secretary shall reconsider the determination in accordance with this paragraph not later than 90 days after the date that such request was received.October 13, 2021

children.label: i
children.text: discharge the Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan under part D, and reinstate the recipient’s grant under this subpart;

children.label: ii
children.text: discharge any interest or fees that may have accumulated during the period that the grant was converted to a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan under part D;

children.label: iii
children.text: if the recipient has other loans under part D, apply any payments made for the Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan under part D during such period to those other loans under part D;

children.label: iv
children.text: if the recipient does not have other loans under part D, reimburse the recipient for any amounts paid on the Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan under part D during such period;

children.label: v
children.text: request that consumer reporting agencies remove any negative credit reporting due to the conversion of the TEACH Grant to a loan; and

children.label: vi
children.text: use the additional information provided under subparagraph (A) to determine the progress the recipient has made in meeting the service obligation.

children.heading: Extension of time to complete service obligation
children.label: C Extension of time to complete service obligation
I
children.text: 8 years; minus

children.label: II
children.text: the number of full academic years of teaching that the recipient completed prior to the reconversion of the loan to a TEACH Grant under subparagraph (B), including any years of qualifying teaching completed during the period when the TEACH Grant was in loan status; and

children.label: ii
children.text: treat any full academic years of teaching described in clause (i)(II) as years that count toward the individual’s service obligation (regardless of whether the TEACH Grant funds were in grant or loan status) if that time otherwise meets the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Change of high-need designation
children.label: 1 Change of high-need designation
children.text: If a recipient of an initial grant under this subpart has acquired an academic degree, or expertise, in a field that was, at the time of the recipient’s application for that grant, designated as high need in accordance with subsection (b)(1)(C)(vii), but is no longer so designated, the grant recipient may fulfill the service obligation described in subsection (b)(1) by teaching in that field.

children.heading: Extenuating circumstances
children.label: 2 Extenuating circumstances
children.text: The Secretary shall establish, by regulation, categories of extenuating circumstances under which a recipient of a grant under this subpart who is unable to fulfill all or part of the recipient’s service obligation may be excused from fulfilling that portion of the service obligation.

children.heading: Communication with recipients
children.label: 3 Communication with recipients
children.text: The Secretary shall notify TEACH grant recipients not less than once per calendar year regarding how to submit the employment certification under subsection (b)(1)(D) and the recommendations and requirements for submitting that certification under subsection (d)(5).

children.heading: Qualifying schools and high-need fields
children.label: 4 Qualifying schools and high-need fields
children.text: The Secretary shall maintain and annually update a list of qualifying schools as described in subsection (b)(1)(B), and a list of high-need fields as described in subsection (b)(1)(C) and shall make such lists publicly available on the Department’s website in a sortable and searchable format.

children.heading: Recommended submissions
children.label: A Recommended submissions
children.text: The Secretary shall notify TEACH Grant recipients that the Department recommends that TEACH Grant recipients submit the employment certification described in subsection (b)(1)(D) as soon as practicable after the completion of each year of service.

children.heading: Required submission
children.label: B Required submission
children.text: A TEACH Grant recipient shall be required to submit to the Department employment certification within the timeframe that would allow that individual to complete their service obligation before the end of the service obligation window.

children.heading: Notification
children.label: C Notification
children.text: The Secretary shall notify TEACH Grant recipients of the required submission deadlines described in this paragraph.

children.heading: Adjustment of deadline
children.label: D Adjustment of deadline
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust the submission deadline described in subparagraph (B) to account for a service obligation window extension.

children.heading: Alternative to certification
children.label: E Alternative to certification
children.text: The Secretary shall provide an alternative to the certification of employment described in subsection (b)(1)(D) for recipients who cannot obtain such required certification of employment from the chief administrative officer of the school because the recipient can demonstrate the school is no longer in existence or the school refuses to cooperate.
